title_en: Scholarly Reception and Influence on Islamic Biographical Literature
title_ar: الاستقبال العلمي وأثره في أدب التراجم الإسلامي
content_en:
Wafayat al-A'yan was recognized almost immediately upon its completion as a work of exceptional quality. Ibn Khallikan's contemporaries praised it for its combination of scholarly reliability and literary elegance, and later scholars drew on it repeatedly as a primary source.

Adh-Dhahabi (d. 748 AH/1348 CE), the great hadith biographer and historian, used Wafayat al-A'yan extensively in his own biographical works, particularly Siyar Alam an-Nubala and Tarikh al-Islam. Adh-Dhahabi sometimes corrected Ibn Khallikan's dates or supplemented his information, but he treated the Wafayat as a reliable primary source worthy of serious engagement. His corrections and additions were themselves a form of tribute to the Wafayat's importance.

As-Safadi (d. 764 AH/1363 CE) produced an important continuation and supplement to the Wafayat called Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat, a much larger biographical dictionary that drew on Ibn Khallikan's work as its model and starting point while expanding the coverage significantly. The existence of this continuation testifies to the Wafayat's status as the defining work in its genre.

Ibn Khallikan's work also attracted attention from European orientalists early in Western engagement with Islamic literature. Baron MacGuckin de Slane produced a complete French translation in four volumes (1842–1871) that made the Wafayat accessible to European scholarship and confirmed its status as a major historical source. This translation was remarkable for its time and demonstrated to Western scholars the richness of the Islamic biographical tradition.

In modern Arab scholarship, the Wafayat has been repeatedly edited and published, with a critical edition by Ihsan Abbas (Beirut, 1968–1972) in eight volumes becoming the standard scholarly reference. This edition, produced with rigorous source verification and extensive annotation, is the edition primarily used in academic research today.

For Islamic historians, the Wafayat remains one of the most cited biographical dictionaries in the literature, surpassed only by adh-Dhahabi's encyclopedic Siyar Alam an-Nubala and Ibn Hajar's Lisan al-Mizan in terms of scholarly citations.
title_id: Penerimaan Ulama dan Pengaruh terhadap Literatur Biografi Islam
content_id:
Wafayat al-A'yan diakui hampir segera setelah selesainya sebagai karya berkualitas luar biasa. Orang-orang sezaman Ibn Khallikan memujinya karena perpaduan keandalan keilmuan dengan keanggunan sastra, dan para ulama kemudian berulang kali mengambil darinya sebagai sumber utama.

Adz-Dzahabi (w. 748 H/1348 M), ahli biografi hadis dan sejarawan besar, menggunakan Wafayat al-A'yan secara ekstensif dalam karya-karya biografinya sendiri, terutama Siyar A'lam an-Nubala dan Tarikh al-Islam. Adz-Dzahabi kadang-kadang mengoreksi tanggal-tanggal Ibn Khallikan atau melengkapi informasinya, namun beliau memperlakukan Wafayat sebagai sumber utama yang dapat diandalkan yang layak mendapat keterlibatan serius. Koreksi dan tambahan beliau sendiri adalah bentuk penghormatan terhadap pentingnya Wafayat.

As-Safadi (w. 764 H/1363 M) menghasilkan kelanjutan dan suplemen penting untuk Wafayat yang disebut Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat, sebuah kamus biografi yang jauh lebih besar yang mengambil karya Ibn Khallikan sebagai model dan titik awalnya sambil memperluas cakupan secara signifikan. Keberadaan kelanjutan ini menjadi bukti status Wafayat sebagai karya terdepan dalam genrenya.

Karya Ibn Khallikan juga menarik perhatian para orientalis Eropa di awal keterlibatan Barat dengan literatur Islam. Baron MacGuckin de Slane menghasilkan terjemahan Prancis lengkap dalam empat jilid (1842–1871) yang membuat Wafayat dapat diakses oleh keilmuan Eropa dan mengonfirmasi statusnya sebagai sumber historis utama. Terjemahan ini luar biasa untuk zamannya dan menunjukkan kepada para ulama Barat kekayaan tradisi biografi Islam.

Dalam keilmuan Arab modern, Wafayat telah berulang kali diedit dan diterbitkan, dengan edisi kritis oleh Ihsan Abbas (Beirut, 1968–1972) dalam delapan jilid menjadi referensi keilmuan standar.
